questions.questionText: A shopping trolley has a mass of 15 kg. A shopper pushes it with a resultant force of 30 N. Calculate the acceleration of the trolley.

steps.description: Recalls F = ma and rearranges to a = F/m

commonErrors: Multiplying F × m instead of dividing

questions.questionText: The same trolley, now loaded with shopping of total mass 25 kg, is pushed with the same force of 30 N. A friction force of 6 N acts on the trolley. Calculate the new acceleration.

commonErrors: Forgetting to add the mass of the shopping to the trolley mass
Not subtracting friction from the applied force

questions.questionText: Mars has a gravitational field strength of 3.7 N/kg. What is the weight of a 50 kg rover on Mars?

commonErrors: Using g = 10 N/kg for Earth instead of 3.7 N/kg for Mars

questions.questionText: A weightlifter raises a 60 kg barbell through a height of 2.0 m. Gravitational field strength g = 10 N/kg. Calculate the work done by the weightlifter.

commonErrors: Using mass (60) instead of weight (600) in the calculation
Forgetting to convert mass to weight first

questions.questionText: A 0.5 kg ball moving at 8 m/s strikes a 1.5 kg stationary ball. After the collision they move together. Calculate their combined velocity after the collision.

steps.description: By conservation, momentum after = 4.0 kg m/s

commonErrors: Forgetting to add masses after collision

questions.questionText: A swimmer pushes backwards on the water with their hands. Using Newton's third law, explain how this allows the swimmer to move forwards.

markScheme.answer: The swimmer pushes backwards on the water. By Newton's third law, the water pushes forwards on the swimmer with an equal and opposite force. This forward force accelerates the swimmer.

steps.description: The swimmer exerts a force backwards on the water
By Newton's third law, the water exerts an equal and opposite force on the swimmer
This reaction force is directed forwards, causing the swimmer to accelerate forward

commonErrors: Not identifying both objects involved in the force pair
